title: 3 Tests to Know If It’s God’s Voice
hook: Is that God—or just your mind? Use these 3 tests in 30 seconds.
script: Is that God—or just your mind? Use these 3 tests in 30 seconds. Test one: Does it align with Scripture? God won’t contradict His Word. Second Timothy 3:16 is your baseline. Test two: What fruit does it grow? Panic and pressure aren’t the Spirit; love, joy, peace, patience are (Galatians 5:22–23). Test three: Does wise counsel confirm it, and do you sense Christ’s peace ruling, not rushing? Proverbs 11:14 and Colossians 3:15. Psych tip: intrusive thoughts are loud and urgent; God’s voice is steady and values-based. Practice P.A.P.—Pause, Ask God, Prove it with these tests. Save this for when the noise gets loud.

title: When God Feels Silent: A Gentle Filter
hook: If God feels silent right now, watch this.
script: If God feels silent right now, watch this. Your anxiety wants instant answers; God often gives clear steps. Here’s a gentle filter. First, truth check: Would the Bible cheer this on? God’s voice never asks you to sin or to abandon love and integrity. Second, fruit check: Does this nudge settle your soul with love, joy, or patience, or does it spike pressure and fear? Third, community check: run it by wise believers and notice if Christ’s peace umpires your heart (Colossians 3:15). Psych help: don’t obey noise; obey values. Pray, Lord, align my steps with Your Word. Replay this the next time you doubt.

title: Stop Overthinking: Hear God Clearly
script: You may be overthinking this. Here’s how to spot God’s voice in the swirl. God speaks consistently; your anxious brain speaks urgently. So ask: Is this consistent with Scripture I can point to, not just a feeling? (2 Timothy 3:16). Next, scan your heart: is there a growing peace, patience, and self-control, or a countdown clock and dread? That’s Galatians 5 talking. Finally, invite perspective: godly counsel plus prayer often turns confusion into clarity (Proverbs 11:14). Then take one small obedient step; values-based action quiets mental loops. Quick reset: Pause, Ask, Prove. Save this and share it with someone who’s stuck.
